workload: adapter versus new-backend surface audit across all Option 3 lanes

repo_state.notes: Coordinator synthesis from validated lane artifacts.

metrics.key_question: Is the high Option 3 surface accidental adapter duplication or expected new-backend scope?

surface_area.rule_ir: expected backend scope: classification found binary-friendly, WCOJ-relevant, rebuild-heavy, container-heavy, and scheduler-sensitive rule families
surface_area.timestamp_model: expected backend scope: schedule lane requires per-rule last-run freshness and row timestamps
surface_area.frontier_model: expected backend scope: trace/progress lane uses frontier lag as the only current overlap safety signal
surface_area.invalidation_model: expected backend scope if owned directly: equality/rebuild lane needs representative rewrites, retractions/reinsertions, rebuild-index scans, and refresh rows; runtime counters are missing
surface_area.container_refresh: expected backend scope if moved: container lane requires same-id dirty_ids and parent-row refresh distinct from changed-id rebuild
surface_area.scheduler_model: expected backend scope if moved: scheduler lane requires full-match materialization, filtering, backoff, scoped scheduler bindings, and delayed firing
surface_area.join_planner: expected backend scope: native strategies pass smoke tests, but WCOJ runtime benchmarking is blocked without datasets
surface_area.provider_boundary: replacement criterion: the new backend must own moved providers rather than mirror native equality/container/rebuild providers

blockers_before_broad_new_backend: instrument rebuild and container paths with row-level counters
prove a vertical slice owns rebuild/scheduler/container semantics without native shadow state
run WCOJ examples on mounted graph datasets and compare against native join plans
prototype a backend-owned provider boundary for the selected slice
measure trace memory and compaction, not only frontier lag
metrics.performance_summary: Planner and WCOJ lanes are correctness/compile probes rather than full runtime evidence; the only measured throughput signal is still the schedule-overlap harness.
metrics.option_implication: Do not build a permanent adapter that duplicates native state. A new backend remains viable if the next vertical slice owns this scope directly and uses native egglog only as an oracle.

verdict: the surface is too high for a permanent adapter, but it is plausible backend scope if the new layer is authoritative rather than mirrored.
